description: In 1511 King Henry VIII of England established a workshop at Greenwich Palace outside London that produced armor for the English court for over a century. It was staffed largely by German craftsmen, among them Jacob Halder, who produced this half-armor around 1588–90. Its fashionable style and lavish decoration indicates that the noble who commissioned it was looking to demonstrate his wealth and status as much as to protect life and limb.
artist_info:
Jacob Halder (English
1558–1608)
Royal Workshops of Greenwich, England
title: Portions of a Field Armor
